James Geraghty Is making for the
Rasher ticket, it has been learned
that the latter has been promised the
congressional nomination. When Cor
poration Counsel Judson was appoint
ed it was with the understanding
that Geraghty was to receive one-half
of the salary.


REPUBLICAN, CAUCUS.


Ankeny republicans throughout the
city have been working hard since
Saturday arranging for the republi-
can caucus. for the republican pri-
maries Wednesday night and for the
republican convention of Friday, at
whic htme 1t is their intention to
put straight republican ticket in the
field. Ed Sanders, prominently men-
toned as republican candidate, and
his friend. Colonel William Ridpath
have been making a personal convass
for committeeman.


The Wilson republicans are holding
back awaiting the result of the demo-
cratic convention. If Rasher Is nom
mated. the program will be to en-
dorse Acuff.


L. S. Potts. secretary-treasurer of
the trades council, called on City
Treasurer Eggleston and requested
the latter to withdraw his support
from the element that is antagonistic
to union labor. Mr.. Eggleston re-
fused. A similar request was made
of Comptroller Daggett with no bet
ter success. Union men are wrathy
over the naming of a slate of nom
union men by Mr.. Daggett at Satur-
days primaries in his home precinct
of Delaware in the Fourth ward.
